ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* Two-letter code for the country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Full name of the country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code for the country */,
  "continent" TEXT /* Continent where the country is located */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "region_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "country_regions" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region */,
  "is_primary" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if this region is the primary region for the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id", "region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_traffic" (
  "traffic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network traffic entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the network traffic data */,
  "bytes" REAL /* Total bytes of network traffic */,
  "clients" INTEGER /* Number of clients */,
  "bytes_upload" REAL /* Bytes uploaded */,
  "bytes_download" REAL /* Bytes downloaded */,
  "traffic_type" TEXT /* Type of network traffic (e.g., web, file transfer) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("traffic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "traffic_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each traffic type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the traffic type */,
  "type_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the traffic type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "traffic_type_data" (
  "traffic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network traffic entry */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the traffic type */,
  "bytes" REAL /* Bytes of network traffic for the type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("traffic_id", "type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clients" (
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each client */,
  "client_name" TEXT /* Name of the client */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the client belongs to */,
  "client_type" TEXT /* Type of client (e.g., individual, organization) */,
  "client_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the client */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_clients_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "client_traffic" (
  "traffic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network traffic entry */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* ID of the client */,
  "bytes" REAL /* Bytes of network traffic for the client */,
  "bytes_upload" REAL /* Bytes uploaded by the client */,
  "bytes_download" REAL /* Bytes downloaded by the client */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("traffic_id", "client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "traffic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network traffic entry */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date of the version */,
  "version_notes" TEXT /* Brief notes about the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_traffic_id FOREIGN KEY ("traffic_id") REFERENCES network_traffic ("traffic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, researcher) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  "user_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "traffic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network traffic entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_traffic_id FOREIGN KEY ("traffic_id") REFERENCES network_traffic ("traffic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "traffic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network traffic entry */,
  "permission_type" TEXT /* Type of permission (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "approval_status" TEXT /* Approval status of the permission (e.g., approved, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_traffic_id FOREIGN KEY ("traffic_id") REFERENCES network_traffic ("traffic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "traffic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network traffic entry */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "report_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_traffic_id FOREIGN KEY ("traffic_id") REFERENCES network_traffic ("traffic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "report_data" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report */,
  "data" TEXT /* Data included in the report */,
  "data_format" TEXT /* Format of the data (e.g., CSV, JSON) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration setting */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration setting */,
  "config_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration setting */,
  "config_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the configuration setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the team */,
  "team_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the team */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "team_members" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the team (e.g., admin, member) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id", "user_id")
)
